nodes.name: initialize_sampler
nodes.description: Sets up the initial state for the NUTS sampler, including the PRNG seed, target log-probability function, initial positions, and tuning parameters.

inputs.name: target
inputs.constraints: The log-probability density function of the target distribution. Must be a pure function (oracle).

inputs.name: initial_positions
inputs.constraints: Starting points for the MCMC chains.

inputs.name: target_accept_p
inputs.constraints: The target acceptance probability for step size adaptation.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: Seed for the pseudo-random number generator.

outputs.name: initial_sampler_state
outputs.constraints: An immutable data structure containing the complete initial state of the sampler, including the PRNGKey, step size, and initial positions.

nodes.type_signature: (target: Callable[[Array], float], initial_positions: Array, target_accept_p: float, seed: int) -> NUTSState
nodes.conceptual_summary: initialize_stochastic_traversal. Configures and encapsulates the initial conditions for a stateful, iterative search or sampling process. It bundles a scalar potential function (oracle), initial search coordinates, a randomness source (PRNG), and adaptive algorithm parameters into a single, immutable state object, preparing for a subsequent traversal of a high-dimensional space.. Applications: Robotics: Initializing a motion planning search where the oracle scores paths based on collision avoidance and energy cost, and initial coordinates represent different starting joint configurations., Materials Science: Configuring a molecular dynamics simulation to find stable protein folds, where the oracle is an energy potential function and initial coordinates are starting atomic positions., Operations Research: Setting up a metaheuristic solver for a logistics problem, where the oracle scores the efficiency of a given solution and initial coordinates are randomly generated starting solutions., Generative Design: Seeding a search for optimal structural designs (e.g., for an airplane wing), where the oracle is a finite element analysis simulation that scores designs based on stress tolerance and weight, and initial coordinates are starting parameters for the geometry.

nodes.name: run_mcmc_sampler
nodes.description: Executes the NUTS MCMC algorithm for a given number of warm-up and collection steps, producing posterior samples.

inputs.name: sampler_state_in
inputs.constraints: The current state of the NUTS sampler.

inputs.name: n_collect
inputs.constraints: Number of posterior samples to generate and collect.

inputs.name: n_discard
inputs.constraints: Number of warm-up (burn-in) samples to discard before collection.

outputs.name: posterior_samples
outputs.constraints: Collected samples from the posterior distribution.

outputs.name: final_sampler_state
outputs.constraints: The final state of the sampler after the run, including the updated PRNGKey.

nodes.type_signature: (sampler_state_in: NUTSState, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> tuple[Array, NUTSState]
nodes.conceptual_summary: iterative_distribution_sampler. Initializes a stochastic walker at a specified point in a multi-dimensional state space. After a number of transient 'equilibration' steps which are discarded, the walker's subsequent positions are recorded for a specified number of iterations. This collected sequence of state vectors serves as a numerical approximation of an underlying target probability distribution.. Applications: Computational Physics: Simulating the conformational states of a complex molecule by sampling from its Boltzmann energy distribution., Robotics: Generating feasible trajectories for an autonomous agent in an environment with obstacles and uncertainty, where the state space represents the agent's configuration., Operations Research: Finding near-optimal solutions to combinatorial problems (e.g., vehicle routing, facility layout) via simulated annealing, where the sampler explores the solution space., Generative Modeling: Training deep generative models by sampling from a latent space to produce novel data instances (e.g., images, text).

nodes.name: create_progress_channels
nodes.description: Initializes sender/receiver pairs for each MCMC chain to communicate progress statistics.

inputs.name: num_chains
inputs.constraints: Number of parallel chains to be run.

nodes.name: spawn_progress_monitor_thread
nodes.description: Launches a background thread that aggregates ChainStats, displays multi-bar progress, and computes real-time diagnostics like average acceptance probability and R-hat.

inputs.name: progress_receivers
inputs.constraints: Receivers for progress updates from each chain.

inputs.name: total_steps_per_chain
inputs.constraints: Total number of steps (n_collect + n_discard) for each chain.

outputs.name: monitor_thread_handle
outputs.constraints: Handle to the spawned monitoring thread.

nodes.name: execute_chains_in_parallel
nodes.description: Spawns a scoped thread for each MCMC chain, running the sampler and piping progress statistics to the monitor thread.

inputs.name: chains
inputs.constraints: Mutable references to chain state objects.

inputs.name: progress_senders
inputs.constraints: Senders for progress updates for each chain.

inputs.name: n_discard
inputs.constraints: Number of warm-up samples to discard.

outputs.name: collected_samples
outputs.constraints: A vector containing the 2D sample tensor from each chain.

nodes.type_signature: (chains: &mut [Chain], progress_senders: Vec<Sender<ChainStats>>, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> Vec<Tensor<B, 2>>

nodes.name: stack_chain_samples
nodes.description: Combines the list of 2D sample tensors from each chain into a single 3D tensor, with the first dimension representing the chain index.

inputs.name: collected_samples
inputs.constraints: A vector of 2D tensors, one for each chain.

outputs.name: posterior_samples
outputs.constraints: A single 3D tensor containing all samples from all chains.

nodes.name: calculate_run_diagnostics
nodes.description: Computes post-run diagnostic statistics, including the Gelman-Rubin convergence diagnostic (R-hat), from the final stacked posterior samples.

outputs.name: run_stats
outputs.constraints: Struct containing summary statistics like R-hat.

nodes.name: join_monitor_thread
nodes.description: Blocks execution until the progress monitor thread finishes, ensuring all progress reporting is complete before returning.

inputs.name: monitor_thread_handle
inputs.constraints: The handle of the thread to wait for.
